page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1.large is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 35 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the ecs.sn1.large in the lower performance tiers. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests scored 1103.98 and 2930.09 respectively, both falling into the bottom 25% category. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block speed of 163.77 GB/sec, while large block read-only performance drops to 20.95 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression benchmarks also register in the bottom 10% of tested systems. Geekbench scores reflect these limitations, with a single-core score of 912.0 and a multi-core score of 1991.0.

Given the dedicated CPU allocation but overall modest benchmark performance, the ecs.sn1.large is best suited for lightweight compute tasks, basic web serving, or small-scale development environments. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment, which may impact overall I/O performance. The 1:2 CPU-to-RAM ratio offers a cost-efficient entry point for workloads that require dedicated CPU cores but do not demand high memory capacity or intensive processing power.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1.large is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark evaluations indicate modest performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores consistently placing the instance in the bottom 10% to 25% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth is limited, particularly for large blocks, which registers at 20.95 GB/sec. Given these performance characteristics and the 2.0 GB per core memory density, the ecs.sn1.large is best suited for lightweight compute workloads, basic web serving, and non-intensive development tasks where dedicated CPU allocation is required but high throughput is not critical.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1.large is a compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for lightweight, non-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and KVM-based virtualization.
tagline: A budget-friendly, compute-optimized instance with dedicated Intel Xeon cores and a balanced resource profile for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Features 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY vCPUs at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for lightweight compute, basic web serving, and development tasks
